title: Astron-6

infobox.value: * Adam Brooks * Jeremy Gillespie * Matt Kennedy * Conor Sweeney * Steven Kostanski

was a [Canadian film](/wiki/Cinema_of_Canada "Cinema of Canada") production and directing company founded in 2007 by Adam Brooks and Jeremy Gillespie.[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2) The company later expanded to include Matt Kennedy, Conor Sweeney and Steven Kostanski, who are now equal partners in the company. Astron-6 is known for producing low-budget, 1980s-centric independent movies that often combine horror with comedy.

["Fake Trailer leads to movie deal for locals"](http://www.winnipegfreepress.com/arts-and-life/entertainment/movies/fake-trailer-leads-to-movie-deal-for-locals-101283154.html). *Winnipeg Free Press*. 2010-08-23. Retrieved 29 December 2013.

["Women in Horror 2013: The Ladies of Astron-6"](https://web.archive.org/web/20131230233005/http://www.fangoria.com/new/women-in-horror-2013-the-ladies-of-astron-6/). Fangoria. Archived from [the original](http://www.fangoria.com/new/women-in-horror-2013-the-ladies-of-astron-6/) on 30 December 2013. Retrieved 29 December 2013.

["No Sleep, No Surrender: The Making of "Father's Day" - Teaser Trailer"](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=riqRwO23GfM). *[YouTube](/wiki/YouTube "YouTube")*. [Archived](https://ghostarchive.org/varchive/youtube/20211221/riqRwO23GfM) from the original on 2021-12-21. Retrieved 14 November 2018.
["Astron-6 Return with "Divorced Dad" Web Series"](https://bloody-disgusting.com/indie/3384006/astron-6-return-divorced-dad-web-series). 2016-03-19. Retrieved 14 November 2018.

Pace, Dave (2 November 2012). ["Manborg Movie Review"](https://web.archive.org/web/20130704051814/https://www.fangoria.com/index.php/reviews/movies/8086-qmanborgq-and-qbio-copq-movie-review). *Fangoria*. Archived from [the original](https://www.fangoria.com/index.php/reviews/movies/8086-qmanborgq-and-qbio-copq-movie-review) on 4 July 2013.

["Astron-6 team tap Kier, Risk for "THE EDITOR""](https://web.archive.org/web/20131230233003/http://www.fangoria.com/new/astron-6-team-tap-kier-risk-for-the-editor). Fangoria. Archived from [the original](http://www.fangoria.com/new/astron-6-team-tap-kier-risk-for-the-editor) on 30 December 2013. Retrieved 29 December 2013.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Canadian film producer

Astron-6

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Industry | [Film](/wiki/Film "Film") |
| Founded | 2007 |
| Defunct | 2020 |
| Headquarters | [Canada](/wiki/Canada "Canada") |
| Key people | * Adam Brooks * Jeremy Gillespie * Matt Kennedy * Conor Sweeney * Steven Kostanski |
| Website | www.astron-6.com |

**Astron-6** was a [Canadian film](/wiki/Cinema_of_Canada "Cinema of Canada") production and directing company founded in 2007 by Adam Brooks and Jeremy Gillespie.[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2) The company later expanded to include Matt Kennedy, Conor Sweeney and Steven Kostanski, who are now equal partners in the company. Astron-6 is known for producing low-budget, 1980s-centric independent movies that often combine horror with comedy.

## Biography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Astron-6&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Biography")]

A planned 2012 documentary *No Sleep, No Surrender*, which detailed the making and production of their 2011 film *[Father's Day](/wiki/Father%27s_Day_(2011_film) "Father's Day (2011 film)")*, was never completed, though a teaser trailer was released.[[3]](#cite_note-3) The follow-up to *Father's Day*, a [Giallo](/wiki/Giallo "Giallo")-inspired horror comedy, *[The Editor](/wiki/The_Editor_(film) "The Editor (film)")*, was directed by Adam Brooks and Matthew Kennedy in 2014.

In 2016, Astron-6 produced the web series *Divorced Dad*. The series was described by the filmmakers as 'a love letter to the endearingly incompetent cable access television shows created by far from screen-ready small town celebrities during the 1980s and 90s'.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

Gillespie and Kostanski directed the 2016 horror film [*The Void*](/wiki/The_Void_(2016_film) "The Void (2016 film)"), intended as a departure from Astron-6's more comedic work. The film showcases more of Kostanski's creature and practical effects as seen in Astron-6's earlier films.

## Filmography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Astron-6&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Filmography")]

* *Astron-6* (2011, compilation DVD)[[5]](#cite_note-5)
* *[Manborg](/wiki/Manborg "Manborg")* (2011)[[6]](#cite_note-6) (Steven Kostanski only)
* *[Father's Day](/wiki/Father%27s_Day_(2011_film) "Father's Day (2011 film)")* (2011)[[7]](#cite_note-7)
* *[The Editor](/wiki/The_Editor_(film) "The Editor (film)")* (2014)[[8]](#cite_note-8)
* *"*W is for Wish" in *[ABCs of Death 2](/wiki/ABCs_of_Death_2 "ABCs of Death 2")* (2014)[[9]](#cite_note-9)[[10]](#cite_note-:0-10)
* *[The Void](/wiki/The_Void_(2016_film) "The Void (2016 film)")* (2016) (Steven Kostanski And Jeremy Gillespie only)
* *[Leprechaun Returns](/wiki/Leprechaun_Returns "Leprechaun Returns")* (2018) (Steven Kostanski only)
* *Divorced Dad* (2019) — 7-episode series[[10]](#cite_note-:0-10)
* *Chowboys: An American Folktale* (2019)[[11]](#cite_note-11)
* *[Psycho Goreman](/wiki/PG:_Psycho_Goreman "PG: Psycho Goreman")* (2020) (Steven Kostanski and Adam Brooks only)
* "Veggie Masher commercial" in "Storm Drain" segment for *[V/H/S/94](/wiki/V/H/S/94 "V/H/S/94")* (2021) (Steven Kostanski and Conor Sweeney only)
* *[Frankie Freako](/wiki/Frankie_Freako "Frankie Freako")* (2024) (Steven Kostanski only)
* *[Deathstalker](/wiki/Deathstalker_(2025_film) "Deathstalker (2025 film)")* (2025) (Steven Kostanski only)
